item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dementia and Alzheimer are things that terrify me. I'd rather die than forget, so this one hits hard. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
She forgot to remember.

Neat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is sweet and melancholy and poetic in a way. Very enjoyable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice! I really like it! +1

item.title: Staf Post - Closed
item.content:encoded:
Noting that a recent edit was reverted for needlessly correcting the correct British-English spelling of 'storey' to the American-English version of 'story'.

Remember to check before you edit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Feexed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This really came together in the addenda. Honestly, I like the descriptions of the -B's more than the notes.

I must have told them before, as they wish me well. I am strangely reminded of his work,

Talking about two different people, or is she that confused?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the new addendum. Too bad I can't upvote again!

27.7/1999

You changed the date format from the third entry. I'm pretty sure it's deliberate (in which case, well done! Subtle way to show that something's not quite right with the poor woman…) but just in case…!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm, this is tough. I like the idea behind this, and liked the version I read in chat. The new final addendum is helpful in many ways, as it reinforces the most important factor in the creation of the anomaly, and makes the emotional angle more explicit. But that's also what is making me wary - the writing there is almost obvious in how manipulative it is intended to be, meaning that the technical workings are showing through a bit. On the other hand, that is a very hard thing to fix (and I don't have any obvious solutions off the top of my head, apart perhaps from removing one or two repetitions of "things"), so I suspect it's not worth messing with now that you've posted and are getting upvotes. No-vote from me for now, but this may well be something that I come back to later, and marvel at why I haven't already upvoted.

item.title: Re: Cool concept
item.content:encoded: That other item's the first thing that popped to mind when I read the brand. Pretty much immediately.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On second thought, I added some stuff that might flesh this out a bit more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is utterly delightful. I like it a lot!

Excellent idea to start the phenomenon with pareidolia, and the following is just as excellent. I don't agree with Abettik: these creatures don't seem 'horrid' to me, rather pathetic and nostalgic of the period of Cho's life when she was able to create. Great job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looked like a cross between Happy Mask Salesman from LoZ Majora's Mask and Yoda from Star Wars to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's like a vague face on the left hand side.

ETA: Apparently it is from a supposedly haunted Spanish house where faces are reported to appear on the floors.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the first image supposed to look like something? Because I'm not seeing it.

item.title: Re: Cool concept
item.content:encoded:
Ironically, I had a concrete backstory in mind, but several possibilities are open in the article. I didn't want to tell the story using logs or the trite "magic thing with a convenient explaining note/interview" either, so I can see why it's ambiguous.

+ show block
– hide block

Either she created the anomaly spontaneously to reflect her mental state, or she deliberately did so in order to preserve it before it deteriorated further. And just how much of her did she manage to preserve anyway?

Also, remember the other item that used Krylon-brand products?

item.title: Cool concept
item.content:encoded:
I like it. Really cool idea and it has legitimate potential. Focus fire on instance B and the origin of instance A. Ask yourself ¨Who, What, When, Where, Why, How?¨ and then tell us.

Staff notice: This user has been banned from the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I really like the direction you took this in. What starts as a simple haunted house turns into such an interesting story. A demented old woman makes an anomaly that involves bringing these horrid, pained suffering creatures into the world for a few seconds, who quickly proceed to have an understandable existential crisis, before quickly dying. It's quite messed up. Great job!

I also like how you managed to keep it so short, and yet tell everything that you needed to, no more, no less.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kind of straightforward. I'm not sure how I feel about this. The degradation of the artist's faculties reflected in the work is interesting. Currently no-voting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First image source.

Second image source. Edited by user hozz8528 (HoZz on #site19).

All images used under the CC 3.0 license. Also, thanks to all the people I've thrown this at in chat! Let's hope this sticks.
